findings.finding: The compiled-parity lane fits entirely in 2 non-frozen files (new compiled-parity.cjs sibling + orchestrator hook at run-skill-benchmark.cjs:219 and :308); the frozen trio stays byte-identical.
findings.evidence: run-skill-benchmark.cjs:30-37 (requires non-frozen executor-dispatch + build-report); run-skill-benchmark.cjs:219 (routeGold attach site); executor-dispatch.cjs:201 (NOT in frozen list); _args.cjs:24-37 (generic parser accepts --compiled-parity with zero edits)
findings.actionable: Author .opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-improvement/scripts/skill-benchmark/compiled-parity.cjs exporting compiledParity({scenario, legacyObserved, skillRoot, skillId}); in run-skill-benchmark.cjs:219 attach row.compiledParity after row.routeGold; add a BLOCKED-BY-COMPILED-DRIFT branch at run-skill-benchmark.cjs:308 returning exit 3.

findings.finding: Vacuous-parity trap: SPECKIT_COMPILED_ROUTING=1 alone is insufficient. resolve.cjs:41-42 requires the per-hub activation manifest to read servingAuthority==='compiled'; otherwise resolveRoute returns null and compiled-route.cjs:39 emits a legacy sentinel that a naive lane reads as 'compiled matches legacy'.
findings.evidence: resolve.cjs:41-42 (flagEnabled AND servingAuthority!=='compiled'); compiled-route.cjs:36-39 (catch→legacy sentinel); .opencode/specs/.../010-live-activation/activation/sk-code/manifest.json shows servingAuthority:'compiled' (so sk-code is the valid pilot; other hubs may still be 'legacy')
findings.actionable: compiled-parity.cjs pre-flight MUST read 010-live-activation/activation/<hub>/manifest.json and treat servingAuthority!=='compiled' as status 'vacuous' (hard fail), never a pass; document the dual gate in the lane README.

findings.finding: Shape impedance: the compiled engine emits targetQualifiedIds + decisionAction + observedIntents (verified in route-gold.typed.json), but the frozen evaluateRouteGold reads observed.observedResources as surface paths (score-skill-benchmark.cjs:903-904). Direct comparison is vocabulary-incompatible.
findings.evidence: route-gold.typed.json:1 (targetQualifiedIds like 'sk-code/code-review/code-review/workflow/review-cache'); score-skill-benchmark.cjs:903-904 (reads observed.observedIntents + observedResources); router-replay.cjs:235-262 buildResourceContract is the existing typed-pair bridge via leaf-resource-contract.cjs
findings.actionable: In compiled-parity.cjs, translate compiled targetQualifiedIds to the legacy observedResources shape via leaf-resource-contract.cjs (dualReadLegacyResource/compositeKey, same library buildResourceContract uses), then synthesize observed and call the frozen evaluateRouteGold for the comparison.

findings.finding: Spec-tree resolver coupling: compiled-route.cjs:16-21 resolves the resolver from inside .opencode/specs/.../011-runtime-engine/lib/resolve.cjs. Specs are mutable planning artifacts; a rename silently breaks the resolver and the catch at compiled-route.cjs:36-39 returns the legacy sentinel, producing vacuous parity. ZERO activation/manifest.json or compiled/route-gold.typed.json live under .opencode/skills/<hub>/ — all 19 + 12 live under specs only.
findings.evidence: compiled-route.cjs:16-21 (path.resolve into specs/); find results: 19 activation/manifest.json + 12 route-gold.typed.json ALL under .opencode/specs/sk-doc/019-.../007-unified-refactor-implementation/, ZERO under .opencode/skills/<hub>/
findings.actionable: compiled-parity.cjs pre-flight asserts the resolver path exists (fs.existsSync) AND skills-tree activation archive exists; recommend a follow-up to copy per-hub activation/manifest.json + compiled/route-gold.typed.json into .opencode/skills/<hub>/activation/ + compiled/ as the durable serving source (closes named gap #4 for the benchmark's reliance).

findings.finding: Hub-type detection (isHubTypeSkill, run-skill-benchmark.cjs:68 keys on hub-router.json) is misaligned with compiled-serving eligibility (keys on activation manifest + advisor-recommend.ts COMPILED_ROUTING_HUBS allowlist). A hub can have --route-gold auto-on while compiled-parity is structurally N/A.
findings.evidence: run-skill-benchmark.cjs:68-70 (isHubTypeSkill=fs.existsSync hub-router.json); 7 hubs have hub-router.json; resolve.cjs:42 serving authority is per-hub and independent
findings.actionable: compiled-parity.cjs returns status enum {match|drift|vacuous|n/a|resolver-missing}; the orchestrator's verdict switch treats only match as pass, drift/vacuous/resolver-missing as failures, and n/a as informational (no verdict impact) so non-compiled hubs don't false-fail.

findings.finding: The sk-design hub catalog needs a dedicated compiled-routing entry because it already owns the hub's manager-shell routing inventory.
findings.actionable: Add a MANAGER SHELL root entry and a template-authored manager-shell/compiled-routing-and-legacy-fallback.md leaf.

findings.finding: Compiled routing belongs inside the existing advisor_recommend feature because compiledRoute is an optional field on that tool's recommendation schema.
findings.actionable: Extend mcp-surface/advisor-recommend.md frontmatter, HOW IT WORKS, implementation anchors, and validation anchors without creating a new root feature.

findings.finding: The feature-flag governance catalog must document compiled-routing polarity and rollback because the live handler directly gates enrichment on SPECKIT_COMPILED_ROUTING.
findings.actionable: Extend the existing system-spec-kit feature-flag-governance root entry and leaf with phased defaults, eligibility, serving status, drift behavior, and the explicit =0 kill switch.

findings.finding: Child-mode catalogs must not repeat compiled-router behavior because compiled routing resolves the hub mode before the selected packet is loaded.
findings.actionable: Exclude mcp-tooling child, sk-design child, and system-deep-loop mode/runtime catalogs from compiled-router catalog edits.

findings.finding: Catalog text must change with the rollout phase because the authoring contract permits only shipped current reality, including explicitly labelled flag-gated compatibility behavior.
findings.actionable: Publish default-OFF wording during P0-P3 and atomically rewrite it to default-ON plus kill-switch semantics at P4.

findings.finding: The twenty-fourth catalog tree uses uppercase FEATURE-CATALOG.md, so canonical lowercase-only discovery silently undercounts the catalog population.
findings.actionable: Make catalog census tooling report case-variant roots and track the mcp-click-up filename migration outside the router-cutover scope.

findings.finding: The current compiled-router source anchor is unsuitable for a durable catalog because it imports its resolver from the mutable spec tree.
findings.actionable: Promote the resolver first and make all new catalog source tables cite the stable runtime path rather than the current spec path.

findings.finding: New-router cases belong in the seven compiled-eligible hub root playbooks plus system-skill-advisor, while child-mode playbooks remain out of scope.
findings.actionable: Add one hub-specific route-follow case to each eligible hub root and centralize flag, fallback, and status mechanics under system-skill-advisor.

findings.finding: The minimal canonical state corpus is four scenarios covering flag-off, flag-on fresh, drift-to-legacy, and causal seven-hub serving status.
findings.actionable: Create a compiled-routing category in the system-skill-advisor playbook with four per-feature files and exact JSON, exit-code, hook, and verdict evidence.

findings.finding: The prompt-time hook cannot machine-enforce compiled routing because its recommendation type and rendered brief discard compiledRoute.
findings.actionable: Carry compiled action, targets, and serving status through the hook subprocess contract and render them into the injected advisor brief before running LUNA scenarios.

findings.finding: The public compiled front door supplies only prompt text, but sk-code surfaceBundle routing requires risk-slice and certificate context, so code-opencode cannot be proven end-to-end through the current interface.
findings.actionable: Extend the runtime request contract with safe composite-routing context and add a LUNA-high case requiring a surfaceBundle containing sk-code:code-opencode.

findings.finding: The sk-doc root playbook still validates the retired flat RESOURCE_MAP router rather than the live eleven-packet hub contract.
findings.actionable: Regenerate or realign the sk-doc root playbook to mode-registry.json and hub-router.json before adding its compiled-routing scenario.

findings.finding: The current legacy sentinel collapses flag-disabled, missing or malformed manifest, legacy authority, and engine failure, so a serving-status playbook cannot classify cause.
findings.actionable: Add a machine-readable per-hub status probe returning compiled-serving, legacy-fallback, or drifted plus a stable cause code, hash, and generation.

findings.finding: Prior LUNA route runs repeatedly exhausted the 150-second ceiling, so model timeout must be separated from routing correctness.
findings.actionable: Pin gpt-5.6-luna with high effort and explicit fast tier, capture stdout and stderr separately, and record infrastructure timeout as SKIP rather than PASS or routing FAIL.

findings.finding: The named verify_alignment_drift.py gate checks language files but does not inspect the Markdown RESOURCE_MAP whose parent-child equality it is expected to enforce.
findings.actionable: Extend the verifier or add a companion check that parses code-opencode RESOURCE_MAP and fails when the sk-code parent projection diverges.

findings.finding: The storage contract permits only a report JSON, its rendered Markdown, and an optional README, so compiled-vs-legacy parity must be embedded in the canonical Lane C report pair rather than archived as a copied spec-tree artifact.
findings.actionable: Add report.compiledRouting to the orchestrator JSON and render that block through build-report.cjs without editing the frozen scorer trio.

findings.finding: The existing baseline label is the frozen legacy pre-optimization anchor and must not be repurposed as the compiled-parity baseline.
findings.actionable: Create immutable router-compiled-parity-baseline and router-compiled-parity-final sibling runs while leaving baseline untouched.

findings.finding: The runtime resolves serving state from 010-live-activation while 006-parent-hub-rollout contains candidate manifests with different authority, so archiving the candidate tree can record non-serving evidence.
findings.actionable: Generate parity reports through the public front door against the active 010-live-activation manifests and store their hashes/status in compiledRouting; never treat 006 candidates as serving proof.

findings.finding: Lane C live reports currently omit the exact model and variant even though the executor selects them, so a live-gpt-5-6-luna-high folder alone cannot prove the required LUNA HIGH subject.
findings.actionable: Persist provider/model, variant, executor, scenario IDs, and run revision in the report JSON or optional per-run README.

findings.finding: Durable benchmark reports serialize an absolute worktree root, making archived evidence dependent on the originating machine and worktree path.
findings.actionable: Emit a repository-relative target path plus immutable source/input digests, and update provenance validation to accept the durable relative form.

findings.finding: The advisor launcher strips SPECKIT_COMPILED_ROUTING before spawning the daemon, so a parent-profile canary cannot activate the live advisor path.
findings.actionable: Add the flag to CHILD_ENV_ALLOWLIST and test parent-to-daemon propagation plus fresh-session adoption.

findings.finding: Both current enablement predicates are fleet-global, so the specified hub-by-hub P4 cannot be performed by flipping those predicates literally.
findings.actionable: Implement a hub-aware tri-state policy where 0 forces legacy, 1 force-enables canary routing, and unset reads per-hub defaultEnabled state.

findings.finding: All seven activation manifests are already compiled-serving, so making an unset flag mean enabled would activate the entire fleet simultaneously.
findings.actionable: Add explicit per-hub default-on cohort state and advance it one hub at a time behind parity and rollback gates.

findings.finding: The existing routing drift workflow does not trigger on activation manifests, compiled runtime files, hub directives, or the advisor launcher.
findings.actionable: Expand path filters and run a manifest-freshness command that fails with re-mint required for the affected hub.

findings.finding: HUB_CHILD is not merely a duplicated eligibility allowlist; it is also the runtime engine-location map, so removing it via manifest eligibility alone would break engine loading.
findings.actionable: Separate eligibility from artifact location and replace both with a durable manifest index plus stable hub-owned engine paths.

findings.finding: The ENV reference needs daemon-propagation and restart semantics in addition to flag polarity because advisor flags only work when forwarded to the resident child.
findings.actionable: Document current default-off, P2 force-on, P4 unset-as-per-hub-default, explicit 0 kill-switch, invalid-value fallback, child allowlisting, and restart requirements.

findings.finding: Each hub directive still encodes exact =1 opt-in semantics and therefore must change in the same commit as that hub enters the default-on cohort.
findings.actionable: Update the seven directives sequentially to describe per-hub default serving and the explicit =0 legacy override, validating each hub before advancing.

findings.finding: The named verifier `verify_alignment_drift.py` is explicitly markdown-blind (support only TS/JS/Python/Shell/Rust/JSON/JSONC; doc line 50-52 says 'does not scan markdown prose'), yet code-opencode/SKILL.md:51 implicitly cites it via 'a drift guard enforces that equality' — the actual guard is `sk-code-router-sync.vitest.ts:119-192`, two scripts away and not cross-referenced from the opencode surface.
findings.actionable: Either rename the inline claim to name `sk-code-router-sync.vitest.ts` and add a back-link from alignment-verification-automation.md §5, or extend verify_alignment_drift.py with a markdown RESOURCE_MAP parser gated on a `--check-router` flag (default off) that invokes router-replay.cjs parseRouter on every SKILL.md under `--root`.

findings.finding: The compiled-router emits `targetQualifiedIds` (e.g. `sk-code/code-review/code-review/workflow/review-cache`) while the code-opencode surface RESOURCE_MAP emits surface-relative paths (e.g. `references/shared/hooks.md`) — no typed-pair contract bridges the two vocabularies, so 015's Phase 3 typed-gold derivation cannot cover the compiled-vs-surface parity it implicitly needs.
findings.actionable: Add a `qualifiedIdToLeaf` reverse-lookup table to `leaf-resource-contract.cjs` that maps each compiled `targetQualifiedId` to the corresponding `leafResourceId` for the same workflow mode, then expose it via `selectResourceContract`. Add a Vitest that loads sk-code's `route-gold.typed.json` and asserts every `targetQualifiedIds` resolves to a leaf in `leaf-manifest.json`.

findings.finding: Two duplicated allowlists for the same 7 compiled-serving hubs (`advisor-recommend.ts:41-49` COMPILED_ROUTING_HUBS Set vs spec-tree `compiled-route.cjs:23-31` HUB_CHILD map) have no cross-check; an 8th hub added to one but not the other causes the advisor to attach a `compiledRoute` field the resolver cannot compute, or vice versa.
findings.actionable: Add a Vitest in `system-skill-advisor/mcp-server/tests/handlers/advisor-recommend.vitest.ts` that requires the spec-tree's `compiled-route.cjs` and asserts `Array.from(COMPILED_ROUTING_HUBS).sort().join(',') === Object.keys(require(...).HUB_CHILD).sort().join(',')`. Fail with P0 message naming the diverging hub. Add the same check to the existing 014-benchmark-alignment parity harness.

findings.finding: `hub-router.json:32-36` declares code-opencode with a single resource `code-opencode/SKILL.md`, so when router-replay loads the hub router directly (e.g. for a non-OPENCODE-detected task that scores code-opencode intent), it returns only the SKILL.md pointer — bypassing the per-intent RESOURCE_MAP union that the parent surface router would have assembled via `loadSurfaceRouter`. The hub-router level is therefore lossy by design.
findings.actionable: Document the hub-router lossiness in `hub-router.json:1-15` (routerPolicy.outcomes surfaceBundle note), and add an `it()` to `sk-code-router-sync.vitest.ts` that asserts `hub-router.json[v].resources` is a SUBSET of `loadSurfaceRouter(SKCODE).resourceMap[v] || []` for every mode — i.e. hub-router never widens beyond the surface router.

findings.finding: Three disjoint drift guards (`verify_alignment_drift.py` code-files, `verify_stack_folders.py` language folders, `sk-code-router-sync.vitest.ts` Markdown RESOURCE_MAP) cover code-opencode with zero overlap and no orchestrating contract; a regression in any layer is caught only by the matching guard, and the named 'alignment verifier' in the surface contract documents only the first.
findings.actionable: Add a single orchestration script at `.opencode/skills/sk-code/code-opencode/assets/scripts/run-all-drift-guards.sh` that invokes all three guards and exits non-zero on any failure; reference it from `SKILL.md:163` (Surface Standards) so the verification gate lists three concrete commands, not just `verify_alignment_drift.py`.

findings.finding: The committed code-opencode benchmark `targetSkill.root` carries a stale worktree absolute path (`/Users/.../0030-018-rust/...`) from a prior iteration, so the archived evidence depends on a machine + worktree path that no longer exists in the current tree — confirms F-4-5 and extends it to a benchmark report that is the canonical router-mode artifact for the surface.
findings.actionable: Strip the absolute `root` from the committed report JSON and replace it with a hub-relative `.` token, or add a `rootRel` field computed at run time; update `score-skill-benchmark.cjs` (one of the frozen trio — propose a 4th file `build-report.cjs` if edit is forbidden) to normalize the path before serializing. Document the contract in `benchmark/README.md`.

findings.finding: code-opencode has a populated leaf-manifest at `.opencode/skills/sk-code/leaf-manifest.json:4-71` (67 leaves under workflowMode=code-opencode) but the surface RESOURCE_MAP paths inside code-opencode/SKILL.md are not packet-qualified — the typed-pair derivation must strip and re-prefix them, with no test asserting the prefixing matches `registryPacketRoots`.
findings.actionable: Add an `it()` to `sk-code-router-sync.vitest.ts` (under the existing `describe('sk-code surface children own the parent projection')`) that loads the leaf-manifest, extracts the code-opencode leaves, and asserts every leaf path matches `code-opencode/<RESOURCE_MAP entry>` after normalization. Fails if a code-opencode RESOURCE_MAP entry is not represented in the manifest or vice versa.

findings.finding: A Lane C scenario can load with only id yet carry no routing gold, because the frozen loader admits any of id, expected_intent, or expected_resources while the standard validator does not require the latter fields.
findings.actionable: Add a non-frozen authoring or CI validator requiring id, expected_intent, expected_resources, stage, and a parseable exact prompt on every new compiled-routing scenario.

findings.finding: The documented root-playbook validation bypasses the dedicated playbook contract by forcing generic reference validation.
findings.actionable: Validate roots as playbook, make root auto-detection return playbook, and directly validate each newly authored scenario leaf as playbook_feature.

findings.finding: The catalog snippet template says only Automated test and Manual playbook are valid test types, while the validator accepts twelve canonical types.
findings.actionable: Align the template guidance and validator taxonomy before authors choose compiled-routing validation anchors.

findings.finding: Compiled-routing parity evidence must be rendered from report JSON because skill-benchmark-report.md has no authoring template and is renderer-owned.
findings.actionable: Implement the planned compiledRouting JSON-to-Markdown block in build-report.cjs and cover it with a rendered-report fixture test; do not hand-author per-run reports.

findings.finding: The hub catalog entry must change from opt-in/default-off wording to default-compiled plus explicit kill-switch wording in the same P4 stage as that hub's directive.
findings.actionable: Add one phase-specific Compiled Hub Routing root entry and leaf, and gate its post-P4 wording on that hub passing parity, serving-status, fallback, and rollback checks.

findings.finding: An isolated compiled-routing leaf is invalid because the catalog contract requires a canonical root inventory with one linked root entry per per-feature file.
findings.actionable: For hubs without a complete root catalog, either create the full inventory package or keep compiled-routing documentation on the central advisor surface; never create an orphan or single-feature pseudo-catalog.

findings.finding: The existing advisor_recommend feature entry omits the optional compiledRoute response field and its exact flag, allowlist, sentinel, and failure conditions.
findings.actionable: Update advisor-recommend.md HOW IT WORKS and validation anchors to state when compiledRoute is attached and when it is intentionally absent.

findings.finding: The per-feature template claims feature-catalog trigger_phrases drive advisor routing, but the live doc-frontmatter harvester scans only references and assets markdown.
findings.actionable: Extend doc-trigger harvesting to canonical feature-catalog leaves or amend the template so authors do not rely on trigger metadata that the advisor never reads.

findings.finding: The compiled-routing leaf must describe operator-visible behavior, stable implementation anchors, automated tests, and manual-playbook links while keeping scenario execution detail outside the catalog.
findings.actionable: Standardize the leaf as routing-and-activation/compiled-hub-routing.md with Core Behavior, Activation And Rollback, and Observability And Failure Modes content plus stable source and validation tables.

findings.finding: The live playbook executor never explicitly enables SPECKIT_COMPILED_ROUTING for its child process, so a LUNA run cannot deterministically exercise the compiled path.
findings.actionable: Add a new non-frozen compiled-route adapter that spawns the public front door with child-only SPECKIT_COMPILED_ROUTING=1 and asserts serving authority, action, and ordered targets against route gold.

findings.finding: Live Lane C scores the model's declared routing JSON and observed skill calls, not a compiled-router decision, so LUNA agreement cannot prove compiled serving.
findings.actionable: Define each compiled-routing playbook case as a two-plane gate: direct compiled-route CLI parity plus a separately recorded LUNA High routed-subject result.

findings.finding: LUNA High selection is process-wide environment configuration rather than per-scenario data, while --executor selects only the Codex versus OpenCode transport.
findings.actionable: Add an orchestrator-owned scenario execution map with providerModel=openai/gpt-5.6-luna and variant=high, consumed by the new adapter without editing the frozen scenario loader.

findings.finding: Lane C writes its JSON and rendered Markdown only under the caller-provided outputs directory, so durable LUNA evidence requires an explicit hub benchmark path.
findings.actionable: Require the LUNA compiled-routing runner to use .opencode/skills/<hub>/benchmark/live-gpt-5-6-luna-high/ and persist model, variant, compiled observation, and parity verdict in its canonical report pair.

findings.finding: SessionSnapshot.routingRecommendation is actually code-search guidance and has no skill-router authority state, creating a misleading field collision during cutover.
findings.actionable: Rename it to codeSearchRecommendation and add a separate typed skillRouterStatus field.

findings.finding: session_bootstrap exposes skill-graph topology but no live per-hub compiled-versus-legacy serving status.
findings.actionable: Add a live skillRouterStatus payload section with flag source, daemon-observed value, cohort, manifest authority, effective authority, and fallback cause.

findings.finding: The resume workflow may declare context sufficient before probing the current routing posture because its early-stop contract only requires task-continuity essentials.
findings.actionable: For router-cutover packets and P4 actions, require a live router-status probe after packet recovery and before the sufficiency early-exit.

findings.finding: Stop-hook autosave sends generic continuity observations through generate-context rather than preserving decision-time compiled-routing evidence.
findings.actionable: Emit bounded route telemetry when advisor decisions occur and let Stop flush it separately; do not store effective runtime authority as canonical continuity.

findings.finding: Session priming restores the last packet through memory_context without first surfacing whether the restarted runtime is compiled-serving or legacy-serving.
findings.actionable: Include the live session_bootstrap skillRouterStatus section in startup and resume prime output.

findings.finding: The bridge `buildNativeBrief` rebuilds the recommendations list (mk-skill-advisor-bridge.mjs:540-551) without copying the `compiledRoute` field that `enrichCompiledRoutes` just attached (advisor-recommend.ts:357-374), so the bridge is a second compiledRoute-strip layer that destroys machine-enforcement before the plugin even sees it.
findings.actionable: Either copy `compiledRoute` into the synthetic recommendation in buildNativeBrief (preserving the schema field), or thread a top-level `metadata.compiledRouteSummary` through `response()` so the plugin's existing metadata channel (already preserved at line 97-99) can surface it.

findings.finding: The bridge's `CHILD_ENV_ALLOWLIST` (mk-skill-advisor-bridge.mjs:58-87) also lacks `SPECKIT_COMPILED_ROUTING`, so the CLI fallback path (`buildCliBrief` → `runCliRecommend` at line 672-735) spawns a child without the flag even if the parent set it; this is a second env-strip layer beyond the launcher strip already documented in F-5-1.
findings.actionable: Add `SPECKIT_COMPILED_ROUTING` to the bridge allowlist at line 58-87 alongside the other `SPECKIT_ADVISOR_*` entries so the CLI subprocess can read the flag; verify the in-process native path also reads it (it does at line 459 via `env[DISABLED_ENV]` pattern).

findings.finding: `SPECKIT_COMPILED_ROUTING` is not documented in `system-spec-kit/mcp-server/ENV-REFERENCE.md` despite being a load-bearing runtime flag with default-OFF/ON semantics and per-hub interaction — operators have no authoritative reference and no propagation guidance.
findings.actionable: Add an entry with default=OFF, type=boolean, source `.opencode/bin/compiled-route.cjs:10` and `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp-server/handlers/advisor-recommend.ts:362`, plus a propagation note that both the launcher and bridge allowlists must include it (per F-8-2).

findings.finding: No observability surface ships a per-hub serving-authority readout: `spec_kit_skill_advisor_status` (mk-skill-advisor.js:826-861) and `advisor_status` (handlers/advisor-status.ts) never read the 7 manifests at `.opencode/specs/.../010-live-activation/activation/<hub>/manifest.json`, so ADR-002's data-driven eligibility has no operator-visible verification channel.
findings.actionable: Add a `compiled_routing_status` MCP tool (or extend `advisor_status` with a `compiledRouting.hubs: [{hubId, servingAuthority, generation, manifestMtime}]` field) that reads each hub's manifest via the same canonical ACTIVATION_ROOT path resolve.cjs:19 uses.

findings.finding: `enrichCompiledRoutes` invokes `execFileSync` per recommendation (advisor-recommend.ts:332-345) — up to 3 fresh Node process spawns per advisor_recommend call with 5s timeout, no batching, no in-process cache — compounding the F-2-4 spec-tree resolver cost on the hot path.
findings.actionable: Either add a `--multi --hubs <csv>` mode to compiled-route.cjs that batches per prompt, or import the resolver in-process (after ADR-003 promotion to `.opencode/bin/lib/`), or memoize compiledRoute results per (hubId, prompt) per process with a short TTL.

findings.finding: The 009-non-hub-rollout Phase Documentation Map (spec.md:25-28) lists only `001-sk-git/` as a child, but the directory contains 4 children with full packet docs (001-sk-git, 002-system-code-graph, 003-system-skill-advisor, 004-system-spec-kit) and `mcp-code-mode` has no entry at all — 3 children undocumented, 1 candidate missing.
findings.actionable: Update the Phase Documentation Map table to list all 4 directories in on-disk order, add `005-mcp-code-mode` if a child is created or document the omission as a deliberate skip in 012-default-on-decision/decision-record.md ADR-002 consequences.

findings.finding: ADR-002 (decision-record.md:153-252) claims the derived eligibility predicate 'scales to any number of future skills with no code edit per skill' (line 235), but the 5 non-hub single-skill routers (sk-git, system-spec-kit, mcp-code-mode, system-code-graph, system-skill-advisor) have no manifest and no eligibility clause, so they are permanently ineligible by absence — the ADR over-promises without a non-hub ineligibility clause.
findings.actionable: Add a 'Non-hub routers are ineligible by design' clause to ADR-002 consequences explaining that registry-less single-skill routers are intentionally excluded from compiled routing because their typed leaf projection already serves as the authoritative routing contract.

findings.finding: P4 has no gate making compiled output machine-authoritative; the live advisor still attaches it only as optional metadata.
findings.actionable: Add a pre-P4 gate naming the compiled-decision consumer and proving downstream targets originate from compiled output while remaining byte-identical to legacy.

findings.finding: The planned graph is cyclic because P2 needs a parity harness, P3 waits for P2, and the benchmark harness waits for P3 eligibility.
findings.actionable: Move the shared read-only eligibility/status interface to P0, implement Lane C in P1, and reserve P3 for consumer adoption.

findings.finding: The benchmark contract forces flag value 1 and therefore cannot verify the P4 unset-default or explicit-zero kill-switch semantics.
findings.actionable: Add explicit-on, unset-default, explicit-off, and invalid-value environment cases to the parity harness and canonical report.

findings.finding: Manifest-derived eligibility lacks an explicit archetype boundary even though four non-hub routers use a different activation contract and remain legacy-authoritative.
findings.actionable: Type eligibility by canonical store and parent-hub archetype, add negative fixtures for all four non-hubs, and record their explicit legacy or follow-up disposition.

findings.finding: P4 lockstep names hub directives but omits the create-skill templates that deliberately encode exact default-off wording.
findings.actionable: Include both parent-hub templates, create-skill documentation, and generated-fixture tests in each P4 polarity change.

findings.finding: The enumerated P4 gate can pass without completed feature catalogs, manual playbooks, durable benchmark evidence, or LUNA verification.
findings.actionable: Add a P3 coverage-closure join gate requiring validated catalog/playbook artifacts, canonical Lane C report pairs, LUNA evidence, and completed sibling implementations before P4.

findings.finding: The benchmark alignment explicitly excludes real models, so deterministic parity and the required LUNA HIGH routed-subject proof need separate gates that join before cutover.
findings.actionable: Require both offline Lane C parity and a compiled-serving LUNA HIGH playbook run, with transport timeout classified separately, before promotion.

findings.finding: HUB_CHILD is a runtime engine-dispatch table, not merely the duplicate eligibility allowlist ADR-002 assumes it is.
findings.actionable: Separate manifest-derived eligibility from engine discovery, standardize one per-hub engine entrypoint, then remove HUB_CHILD only after all hubs use that contract.

findings.finding: Copying resolve.cjs alone cannot remove spec-tree coupling because its activation root and compiled engine dependency closure are both resolved relative to the current spec directories.
findings.actionable: Promote the resolver, engine loader, activation state, and compiled per-hub bundles together so runtime imports and reads never enter .opencode/specs.

findings.finding: The current serving manifest lacks the hub identity, engine locator, and source-hash data required to implement valid-fresh-manifest eligibility without another mapping.
findings.actionable: Version the runtime manifest with hubId, bundle identity, selected-policy identity, and live-input hashes, then make one shared validator serve advisor, resolver, onboarding, and benchmark consumers.

findings.finding: verify_alignment_drift.py has no cross-file contract mechanism capable of checking eligibility equality, resolver-copy freshness, or forbidden runtime-to-spec imports.
findings.actionable: Add a compiled-routing contract helper invoked by the named verifier, with fixtures for consumer parity, bundle freshness, and zero runtime imports from .opencode/specs.

findings.finding: The compiled engine cache is keyed only by hub ID and therefore would retain a stale snapshot if the promoted resolver becomes long-lived across a manifest re-mint.
findings.actionable: Key or invalidate cached engines by selected-policy hash and source signature, and add a same-process re-mint test before moving compiled routing in-process.

findings.finding: The Lane C verdict enum has no degraded/expected slot for drift-to-legacy fallback, so the new compiledRouting parity gate will collapse three observably distinct states (compiled-serving-with-route-gold-violation, drifted-legacy-fallback, broken-resolver) into one BLOCKED signal — directly violating 012 REQ-004 which mandates drift be scored as degraded/expected.
findings.evidence: score-skill-benchmark.cjs:1765-1773 (verdict enum); 012 spec.md:99+133 (REQ-004 three-state observability contract); 014 spec.md:134-136 (compiledRouting gate OR-combined with routeGold.gate.failed)
findings.actionable: Add a distinct DEGRADED|LEGACY-FALLBACK|DRIFTED sub-verdict to the Lane C enum and have compiledRouting.blockedReason route to it instead of being OR-combined with routeGold.gate.failed; require a regression test asserting the three states produce distinct verdict values.

findings.finding: reduceRouteTelemetry emits telemetryMissingRate/routeMissRate/aliasMissRate/bundleMissRate for the legacy replay only; the planned compiledRouting block is a sibling block, not a parallel compiled.telemetryMissingRate row, so an operator reading report.advisorySignals.routeTelemetry cannot distinguish a hub whose compiled path is silently failing from one whose compiled path is healthy.
findings.evidence: score-skill-benchmark.cjs:745-791 (reduceRouteTelemetry operates on routerResult.routeTelemetry from line 1541); 014 spec.md:107 (compiledRouting as sibling report block, not additive routeTelemetry row)
findings.actionable: Extend reduceRouteTelemetry to a compiledTelemetry parallel block keyed by the compiled front-door output, and surface compiledTelemetry.routeMissRate as an advisory signal so a drift never reads as a healthy legacy number.

findings.finding: No CLI ships a per-hub serving-status readout. 012 REQ-004 mandates it (compiled-serving | legacy-fallback | drifted) and 012 tasks.md:86 (T007) is open, but no module path exists; the seven activation manifests are only inspectable by cat'ing seven files, and compiled-route.cjs resolves (hub,prompt) -> decision rather than fleet -> status[].
findings.evidence: 012 tasks.md:86 (T007, [ ]); .opencode/bin/ contains no compiled-status.cjs (verified via ls); advisor-status.ts:230-338 never reads activation manifests (verified by read); spec_kit_skill_advisor_status never reads activation manifests (F-8-4 prior iteration)
findings.actionable: Ship .opencode/bin/compiled-route-status.cjs with --hub <id> and --all modes reading the seven activation manifests; emit {hubId, servingAuthority, shadowOnly, selectedPolicy.generation, effectivePolicyHash, fenceEpoch, manifestFingerprint} as a stable JSON contract that 014's compiledRouting parity lane can consume as the authoritative status probe referenced at 014 spec.md:103.

findings.finding: isHubTypeSkill (run-skill-benchmark.cjs:68-70) keys route-gold auto-on by hub-router.json file existence, NOT by compiled-eligibility (which requires entry in COMPILED_ROUTING_HUBS at advisor-recommend.ts:41-49 AND a manifest at 010-live-activation/activation/<hub>/manifest.json); a hub-type-but-not-compiled-eligible scenario would route-gold ENFORCED while compiledRouting auto -> off, and operators must reason about both flags to know what was scored.
findings.evidence: run-skill-benchmark.cjs:68-70 (isHubTypeSkill = fs.existsSync hub-router.json); advisor-recommend.ts:41-49 (COMPILED_ROUTING_HUBS = {sk-code, mcp-tooling, system-deep-loop, cli-external-orchestration, sk-prompt, sk-design, sk-doc}); resolve.cjs:42 (servingAuthority derived from manifest, not hub-router.json); 014 spec.md:242 (compiledRouting flag mirrors route-gold)
findings.actionable: Add a compiledEligibility field to routeGold.summary that explicitly distinguishes hub-type (file existence) from compiled-eligible (manifest + allowlist) so the verdict block can render 'hub-type-but-not-compiled-eligible' rather than silently scoring under either gate.

findings.finding: routeGold.gate.failed (score-skill-benchmark.cjs:1767) is the only blockable verdict that consumes a per-hub mechanism, but the planned OR-combination with compiledRouting.gate.failed in 014 will collapse three observably distinct states into one BLOCKED signal — an operator triaging a P4 rollout cannot tell route drift from manifest churn from engine throw without opening JSON, which defeats the per-hub observability 012 REQ-004 mandates.
findings.evidence: score-skill-benchmark.cjs:1764-1773 (verdict switch); 014 spec.md:174 ("drift is reported, never repaired automatically" but no distinct verdict); 012 spec.md:198 ("drifted hub vs broken resolver: both currently read identically; P0 observability must separate degraded/expected (drift) from broken (error)")
findings.actionable: Encode compiledRouting state as a sub-cause of the verdict (compiled-serving | legacy-fallback-drifted | broken-compiled-path | drift-falls-back-to-legacy) and require the verdict switch to inspect compiledRouting.subVerdict BEFORE deciding BLOCKED-vs-CONDITIONAL-vs-DEGRADED; add a vitest asserting the four sub-causes produce four distinct outer verdicts.

findings.finding: The frozen-scorer pin is duplicated byte-for-byte across three callers — lib/activate-hub.cjs:46-49, lib/flip-serving.cjs:42-45, and the planned compiled-routing-parity.cjs (014 REQ-001) — with no shared source of truth; an intentional pin update can pass activation while parity silently fails (or vice versa), surfacing two different error messages for the same drift.
findings.evidence: lib/activate-hub.cjs:46-49 (PINNED_SCORER_DIGESTS hardcoded); lib/flip-serving.cjs:42-45 (same three names + digests); 014 spec.md:132 (REQ-001 plans third copy)
findings.actionable: Extract the three digests into .opencode/bin/compiled-routing-scorer-pins.json and require() it from all three callers; a drift on any digest fails all three with the same error string naming the same file, so a future pin release cannot desync activation and parity.

findings.finding: The standard per-scenario template omits the typed manifest gold required by topology validation, so newly authored compiled-routing scenarios default to legacy-only gold.
findings.actionable: Add expected_workflow_mode and typed expected_leaf_resources to the manifest-backed Lane C template path and require strict topology validation for benchmarked hubs.

findings.finding: The topology validator treats quoted typed workflow and leaf values as literal quotes while the Lane C loader accepts quoted YAML, creating a copyable-template failure mode.
findings.actionable: Make topology parsing quote-tolerant, document one canonical typed-gold serialization, and test both quoted and unquoted forms.

findings.finding: A report with no typed-gold exclusions bypasses renderer provenance validation and is still emitted as JSON plus renderer-owned Markdown, so archival output alone cannot prove typed compiled parity ran.
findings.actionable: Require populated compiledRouting parity metadata in report JSON before archiving the P4 evidence and render that state into the Markdown report and benchmark index.

findings.finding: No serving-snapshot.json schema exists per hub that joins manifest + fence + flip + live hash + flag + parity into one durable, machine-readable artifact; the current per-hub evidence is split across activation-record.json, fence-state.json, manifest.json, and serving-flip-record.json under the spec tree.
findings.actionable: Define and ship a serving-snapshot.json schema (hubId, capturedAt, flag, manifest{selectedPolicyHash,generation,fenceEpoch,servingAuthority,shadowOnly}, liveConfigHash, freshness, engineResolverPath, parityBaseline, realModelLast) and emit a renderer for serving-snapshot.md under sk-doc/create-benchmark; reference it from create-benchmark §10 storage guide and create-manual-testing-playbook run-archive convention.

findings.finding: No CLI/MCP/plugin module path is named for the fleet-wide per-hub serving probe, so 012 spec.md:133 / plan.md:158 / tasks.md:86 T007 (per-hub compiled-serving | legacy-fallback | drifted readout) has no concrete implementation surface today.
findings.evidence: .opencode/bin/compiled-route.cjs:23-40 (resolves only (hub,prompt)->decision; --fleet flag absent); 011-runtime-engine/lib/resolve.cjs:22-48 (returns single null/decision, no fleet function); .opencode/plugins/mk-skill-advisor.js:826-861 (status tool emits cache stats, never reads the 7 manifests)
findings.actionable: Add a compiledRoutingFleetStatus block to spec_kit_skill_advisor_status (~10 lines) that reads each of the 7 manifests, recomputes each live hash via loadHubEngine(), and emits hub_serving[] plus a fleet_summary row count per state; back it by a new .opencode/bin/compiled-route.cjs --fleet mode that the plugin can shell out to.

findings.finding: The 014 Lane C compiled-routing-parity.cjs is planned to write JSON+Markdown to caller-provided --outputs-dir but no spec names a durable hub-local archive location, so a LUNA-high routed-subject run would either pollute the parity dir or vanish after the run.
findings.evidence: .opencode/specs/sk-doc/019-sk-doc-router-alignment/020-router-unification-program/007-unified-refactor-implementation/014-benchmark-alignment/spec.md:79,109-118 (creates compiled-routing-parity.cjs, modifies run-skill-benchmark.cjs and build-report.cjs, no benchmark/ path declared); sk-code/benchmark/README.md:73-83 (Lane C convention = sibling <run-label>/ folders)
findings.actionable: Add a 'Report Path Convention' requirement to 014 spec naming <hub>/benchmark/compiled-routing/<run-label>/{skill-benchmark-report.json,skill-benchmark-report.md} as the durable sibling of router-final/ and live-final/; update the seven hub benchmark/README.md index rows with the new current run.

findings.finding: Both activate-hub.cjs and flip-serving.cjs overwrite their singular audit files (activation-record.json, serving-flip-record.json), silently destroying re-mint history; system-deep-loop already exhibits this with manifest.json fence=3 hash 82d7f3... versus serving-flip-record.json fence=3 hash 7b5491... — a re-mint with no audit row.
findings.actionable: Add append-only flip-history.jsonl per hub (lines: at,fenceEpoch,kind{activate|flip|re-mint},priorHash,newHash,by); switch activate-hub.cjs and flip-serving.cjs to append rather than overwrite; include the last-N entries in serving-snapshot.json so the snapshot is self-auditing.

findings.finding: spec_kit_skill_advisor_status (mk-skill-advisor.js:826-861) already runs at session start, has 22 emitted rows, and is the lowest-blast operator-facing augmentation point for per-hub serving state, yet none of its rows read the 7 activation manifests.
findings.actionable: Augment the execute() body in mk-skill-advisor.js:830 to iterate .opencode/specs/.../010-live-activation/activation/, parse each manifest.json, and append hub_serving[] plus fleet_summary lines to the returned string; keep the function prompt-safe (size cap, no secrets, no spawned processes that block session start).

findings.finding: create-skill (013 spec) plans to mint a manifest for --compiled-routing ready hubs but no spec names where the manifest lives; until the spec-tree 010-live-activation path is populated AND COMPILED_ROUTING_HUBS (advisor-recommend.ts:41) is updated AND the fleet probe is wired, a freshly-scaffolded compiled-ready hub is invisible to every observability surface.
findings.evidence: .opencode/specs/.../013-create-skill-alignment/spec.md:73-79 (--compiled-routing legacy|ready option, mints manifest but location unspecified); system-skill-advisor/mcp-server/handlers/advisor-recommend.ts:41-49 (COMPILED_ROUTING_HUBS hardcoded allowlist); compiled-route.cjs:23-31 (HUB_CHILD dispatch table)
findings.actionable: Author 013 to emit the manifest into BOTH the spec-tree canonical location and a hub-local .opencode/skills/<hub>/activation/manifest.json copy so the fleet probe can scan one location for all known hubs including create-skill output that has not yet been activated.

findings.finding: No plugin or tool wires the runtime front door's (hub,prompt) decision with a session-start fleet probe and writes/updates a per-hub serving snapshot; the mk-dist-freshness-guard.js pattern (compiled-output staleness detection for MCP dist artifacts) lifts directly into a parallel mk-compiled-routing-snapshot.js but no spec names it.
findings.evidence: .opencode/plugins/mk-dist-freshness-guard.js (compiled dist drift detection); .opencode/plugins/README.md (no compiled-routing-snapshot plugin listed); 011-runtime-engine/lib/compiled-route.cjs:33-62 (loadHubEngine + engineCache are runtime-private, not exported for plugins)
findings.actionable: Export loadHubEngine (or a read-only equivalent) from 011-runtime-engine/lib/compiled-route.cjs:87 to enable a session-start plugin; add .opencode/plugins/mk-compiled-routing-snapshot.js that walks the 7 manifests, runs loadHubEngine on each, and writes .opencode/skills/<hub>/benchmark/compiled-routing/latest/serving-snapshot.json with the F-12-1 schema.

findings.finding: manifest.serving-prior.json is captured only on the first flip ever (flip-serving.cjs:125 if-exists guard) and is never refreshed by activate-hub.cjs swapping manifest.json, so a P4a re-mint followed by flip+rollback restores the wrong generation
findings.actionable: Remove the if-exists guard in flip-serving.cjs:125 to unconditionally re-save manifest.serving-prior.json before each forward flip (or have activate-hub.cjs refresh serving-prior at line 214 whenever it swaps manifest.json)

findings.finding: activate-hub.cjs has no operator-facing --rollback command (parseArgs accepts only --hub/--child/--verify/--json) even though flip-serving.cjs implements --rollback, making P4a recovery an unscripted manual file edit
findings.actionable: Add --rollback mode to activate-hub.cjs that swaps manifest.prior.json into manifest.json and rewrites fence-state.json, reusing the byte-exact hash validation already in proveRollback

findings.finding: The production kill-switch is bi-state (literal '1' on, everything else off) at both read sites, so it cannot implement the P4 default-on target that requires distinguishing unset=on from explicit '0'=off
findings.actionable: Convert to tri-state: unset=on (post-P4), '1'=on (explicit), '0'|'false'|'off'=off (explicit kill); update resolve.cjs:23 and advisor-recommend.ts:362 together and add the flag to both CHILD_ENV_ALLOWLIST sets

findings.finding: compiled-route.cjs fails safe to a legacy sentinel on ANY resolver error including a moved or archived spec tree, with no stderr, log, or telemetry — an invisible fleet-wide rollback if 007-unified-refactor-implementation is archived during the 019 program cutover
findings.actionable: Emit a single stderr breadcrumb inside the catch block at compiled-route.cjs:36-38 (e.g. 'compiled-route: resolver unavailable for <hub>, using legacy') so operators can grep for silent fail-safes

findings.finding: The fence epoch is incremented on both forward flip and rollback (flip-serving.cjs:128 and 105), so the fence value alone cannot tell an operator whether the current state was reached by cutover or by recovery — defeating P4 audit reconstruction
findings.actionable: Add a direction field ('forward'|'rollback') to fence-state.json writes, or stop advancing the fence on rollback so manifest.json.fence can be reconciled against serving-flip-record.json.fenceEpoch.before

findings.finding: The launcher's .env/.env.local loader (mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs:70-76) runs at module top-level and injects into process.env, but createChildEnv() (line 267) filters through CHILD_ENV_ALLOWLIST (line 99-150) which omits SPECKIT_COMPILED_ROUTING, so the standard 'put it in .env' operator path silently strips the flag from the spawned advisor child
findings.actionable: Add SPECKIT_COMPILED_ROUTING to both CHILD_ENV_ALLOWLIST sets (launcher line 99-150 and bridge mk-skill-advisor-bridge.mjs:58-87) and document the .env propagation contract in ENV-REFERENCE.md

findings.finding: Lane C live mode does not execute the manual playbook command, evidence, or pass-fail contract because sk-doc scenarios are loaded with null pass criteria and noncritical status.
findings.actionable: Add a non-frozen cutover playbook executor or evidence collector that runs each authored command sequence and gates on captured expected signals.

findings.finding: The playbook skill limits scenario verdicts to PASS, FAIL, and blocked SKIP while its root template permits PARTIAL and can declare READY with noncritical partial scenarios.
findings.actionable: Unify the template, validator, and skill on one verdict enum and require every compiled-router cutover scenario to be critical and PASS.

findings.finding: The current live executor dispatches a reasoning variant but records only the model, so archived output cannot prove the required GPT-5.6 LUNA HIGH configuration.
findings.actionable: Add an execution-context block containing executor, exact model, variant, CLI version, flag state, runtime digest, and manifest digest to every live report.

findings.finding: The benchmark runner overwrites an existing caller-selected report pair even though the durable storage contract declares every run label immutable.
findings.actionable: Fail closed when a run-label directory or report pair already exists and create every cutover run under a unique indexed sibling label.

findings.finding: Existing real-model acceptance declares completion despite LUNA transport failures, so zero wrong-hub routes is not an all-LUNA-pass criterion.
findings.actionable: Require two clean successful LUNA HIGH observations per selected prompt and treat every timeout or missing observation as incomplete until rerun.

findings.finding: Prior LUNA routing evidence contains no gold-bearing holdouts and therefore establishes fitted-prompt behavior rather than routing generalization.
findings.actionable: Reserve at least one gold-bearing held-out paraphrase per eligible hub and keep its expected route outside the model prompt.

findings.finding: The raw log records a completed post-flip sweep while the sibling runtime summary still says that sweep was not rerun.
findings.actionable: Derive completion metadata from one canonical evidence ledger and block P4 while packet summaries contradict recorded execution receipts.

findings.finding: Six of the seven compiled-eligible hubs lack the canonical hub-root catalog required before a compiled-routing leaf can be added without creating a misleading partial inventory.
findings.actionable: Create complete canonical hub-root catalog packages for sk-code, mcp-tooling, system-deep-loop, cli-external-orchestration, sk-prompt, and sk-doc, then add one routing leaf to all seven hubs.

findings.finding: The exact per-feature contract is the entire scaffold, including source and validation tables, rather than a root-catalog paragraph or abbreviated router note.
findings.actionable: Create one compiled-route-enrichment leaf for the advisor and one compiled-mode-routing leaf per eligible hub using the complete scaffold.

findings.finding: Catalog current-reality wording cannot claim default-on before P4 because the shipped advisor still enables enrichment only when SPECKIT_COMPILED_ROUTING equals 1.
findings.actionable: Land opt-in additive wording before cutover and make the default-on plus explicit-zero kill-switch wording a P4 lockstep edit.

findings.finding: Final catalog source tables cannot comply with the stable-source rule while resolver and activation authority still live under mutable spec history.
findings.actionable: Sequence final catalog authoring after resolver promotion and canonical manifest-location work, and cite only durable runtime paths.

findings.finding: The per-feature template claims trigger phrases drive advisor routing, but the live document harvester excludes feature-catalog directories by scanning only references and assets.
findings.actionable: Either remove the routing-effect claim from the catalog template or extend harvesting to feature-catalog with capped scoring and invariance tests.

findings.finding: Existing validation cannot prove catalog completeness and treats off-taxonomy validation types as warnings rather than blocking failures.
findings.actionable: Add a strict package validator covering the router plus seven hubs, root-leaf bijection, source-path existence, and Automated test or Manual playbook taxonomy.

findings.finding: The OpenCode hook drops the advisor's compiledRoute before rendering and injecting its system brief, so default-on cannot make compiled routing effective at that agent boundary.
findings.actionable: Define a validated compiled-decision projection in both native and CLI bridge paths and render or structurally consume route, clarify, defer, and reject outcomes before system-context injection.

findings.finding: The supported bridge-to-launcher fallback has a second child-environment allowlist that omits SPECKIT_COMPILED_ROUTING, so an explicit future =0 kill-switch is lost before the advisor child starts.
findings.actionable: Add the flag to both bridge and launcher child-env allowlists and test unset, 1, and 0 through native plus fallback subprocess chains.

findings.finding: The OpenCode advisor cache fingerprints skill roots and advisor artifacts but not activation manifests, so a serving flip, rollback, or re-mint will not invalidate a future compiled-routing hook decision.
findings.actionable: Include a shared effective-serving-state fingerprint in the cache key or bypass the cache for compiled decisions, with manifest-flip and rollback invalidation tests.

findings.finding: The OpenCode plugin regression suite stubs opaque bridge output and therefore cannot detect compiledRoute loss or kill-switch propagation through the real hook chain.
findings.actionable: Add end-to-end bridge and plugin system-transform tests using a real compiled decision for native and no-dist launcher fallback paths.

findings.finding: Legacy route, holdout, and disambiguation cases are behavior-identical under compiled serving, so the new matrix should test serving authority rather than duplicate routing semantics.
findings.actionable: Add one compiled-routing scenario per eligible hub that compares compiled and legacy targets while varying default-on, explicit =0, drift, and resolver-failure states.

findings.finding: The minimal complete hub matrix is seven primary scenarios selected by distinct route shape: sk-code surfaceBundle, three generic ordered bundles, sk-prompt default routing, and the explicit sk-design and sk-doc bundle rules.
findings.actionable: Create one hub-local compiled-routing scenario file for each of the seven allowlisted hubs and place secondary authority-state checks under Optional Supplemental Checks.

findings.finding: The current playbook evidence contract can pass without proving that compiled routing ran because it records advisor ranking but not compiledRoute, serving authority, flag state, fallback cause, or manifest identity.
findings.actionable: Add compiledRoute, serving-status, flag, fallback-cause, manifest-digest, model, and reasoning-effort fields to the global evidence requirements and every new scenario.

findings.finding: mcp-tooling's Figma-plus-Refero ordered bundle is only a prose supplemental assertion while its primary corpus consists of single routes and defer, so it cannot provide primary compiled bundle evidence.
findings.actionable: Promote the explicit Figma-plus-Refero request into the primary row of the new mcp-tooling compiled-routing scenario and retain ambiguous-defer as a supplemental variant.

findings.finding: sk-prompt advertises orderedBundle while declaring no bundle rules, leaving the branch's reachability undefined and making a required manual scenario impossible to specify deterministically.
findings.actionable: Prove a deterministic dual-intent route and add its rule plus scenario, or remove orderedBundle from the advertised outcomes before cutover.

findings.finding: The canonical playbook validator does not recurse into per-feature files, so it cannot enforce seven-hub matrix completeness or the compiled-routing evidence fields.
findings.actionable: Add a dedicated compiled-routing playbook validator that requires all seven hub scenario IDs, one primary nine-column row per file, and every authority-state evidence field.

findings.finding: The phase graph is cyclic because P2 parity evidence gates P3 while Lane C parity declares P3 eligibility a prerequisite.
findings.actionable: Move the pure eligibility and manifest-discovery API into P0 for benchmark and canary use, while retaining production-consumer replacement in P3.

findings.finding: P4 requires sibling alignment phases only to be available even though both alignment packets explicitly remain Planned and Not started.
findings.actionable: Replace availability with explicit implemented-and-verified artifact, test, archive, serving-status, and rollback gates before P4.

findings.finding: Create-skill requires shared freshness and data-driven discovery and expressly forbids a local eligibility map, making a single authority interface prerequisite to drift CI, benchmarking, and onboarding.
findings.actionable: Give one 015 child ownership of the code-opencode RESOURCE_MAP, alignment verifier, manifest-input derivation, and shared discovery API; require all later children to consume it.

findings.finding: sk-doc assigns catalog scaffolds and benchmark storage indexes to templates but reserves per-run reports for the renderer, so template repair, content population, and run emission are different implementation boundaries.
findings.actionable: Create separate 015 children for sk-doc template-validator alignment, catalog/playbook population, and benchmark execution-archiving, in that order.

findings.finding: P2 asks only to record canary evidence while the durable Lane C contract requires a fresh sibling run folder, frozen baseline, and synchronized index.
findings.actionable: Make immutable hub-local report pairs, index rows, serving snapshots, and model-variant provenance mandatory P2 exit artifacts before P3 begins.

findings.finding: The create-skill implementation cannot start until the P3 minter, freshness predicate, canonical manifest location, and data-driven discovery interfaces stabilize.
findings.actionable: Schedule create-skill onboarding after P3 consumer interfaces stabilize and require its verified ready-hub fixture before P4.

findings.finding: The active 015 research JSONL omits canonical iteration fields required by the deep-research executor contract.
findings.actionable: Add reducer-side canonicalization for type, newInfoRatio, status, and novelty justification before synthesis or dependency automation consumes the lineage.

findings.finding: Lane C guards only the hub-local leaf manifest even though compiled serving is controlled by a separate activation manifest, allowing one parity report to span serving generations.
findings.actionable: Have the new parity harness snapshot and post-verify activation-manifest bytes/digest, authority, policy tuple, and fence; abort rather than archive when any serving identity changes.

findings.finding: Lane C archives an absolute target worktree path as report provenance, which is already stale in the shipped sk-code report and is not validated by the renderer.
findings.actionable: Append orchestrator-owned portable compiledRouting provenance with repo-relative hub root, source revision, activation-manifest digest, policy tuple, front-door digest, and frozen-file hashes; render and validate it from JSON.

findings.finding: The planned compiled-routing-parity CLI control cannot reach the orchestrator through the supported Lane C command because loop-host and both workflows whitelist only the current option set.
findings.actionable: Either add compiled-routing-parity to loop-host, command input/presentation, and both workflow dispatches, or remove the flag design and record an unconditional resolved auto mode in every report.

findings.finding: Hub directives make compiled decisions authoritative while the live advisor preserves legacy recommendations and attaches compiled output only as metadata.
findings.actionable: Choose one serving contract and make hub directives, advisor output, hooks, tests, and catalogs consume compiledRoute consistently before P4.

findings.finding: The documented RESOURCE_MAP equality gate is inert because verify_alignment_drift.py does not scan Markdown or implement RESOURCE_MAP comparison.
findings.actionable: Add a blocking parser that extracts and compares the code-opencode, sibling, and parent RESOURCE_MAP projections, with positive and drift fixtures.

findings.finding: The P4 lockstep set covers seven live directives but omits the second create-skill parent template that will still emit literal-1 and off-by-default wording.
findings.actionable: Add both parent templates to the P4 cutover controller and require normalized parity across both templates and all seven hub SKILL.md directives.

findings.finding: ENV-REFERENCE cannot currently be the compiled-routing flag authority because its generated table is scoped to memory search flags and lacks the advisor flag's literal-1/default-on-kill-switch semantics.
findings.actionable: Add a validated cross-server environment section or broaden generation, then document and test unset, 1, 0, false, and invalid-value behavior.

findings.finding: The P0-P4 runtime cutover has no valid implementation scope because phase 012 is documentation-only while its tasks require runtime, CI, environment, manifest, and hub changes.
findings.actionable: Create or designate scoped implementation children for P0-P4 before modifying runtime surfaces.

findings.finding: The accepted resolver-promotion decision is contradicted by a handoff that reopens guarded spec-tree coupling as an option.
findings.actionable: Make stable-path promotion binding and remove the residual-coupling branch from downstream plans.

findings.finding: The four completed non-hub compiled candidates lack a complete default-on disposition while create-skill plans to reject compiled onboarding for standalone skills.
findings.actionable: Define an explicit non-hub archetype policy covering sk-git, system-code-graph, system-skill-advisor, and system-spec-kit.

findings.finding: The authoritative three-state serving-status taxonomy omits the broken state required by the benchmark contract.
findings.actionable: Define one machine-readable status schema using either four states or separate serving-authority and fallback-cause fields.

findings.finding: Post-cutover behavior for invalid SPECKIT_COMPILED_ROUTING values is unassigned even though the verification matrix requires invalid-value coverage.
findings.actionable: Specify and test a strict unset/0/1/invalid truth table with fail-closed diagnostics.

findings.finding: The P2 canary cannot start because its environment profile and promotion owner remain unnamed.
findings.actionable: Name the canary profile, accountable owner, observation window, promotion thresholds, and rollback trigger.

findings.finding: The required GPT-5.6 LUNA HIGH acceptance has no owner because phase 014 explicitly excludes real-model and network execution.
findings.actionable: Add a separate live acceptance stage that pins GPT-5.6 LUNA HIGH and requires every routed scenario to complete and pass.

findings.finding: Ownership of the non-zero drift signal remains undecided between Lane C and the separate drift-CI job.
findings.actionable: Assign exactly one blocking drift gate and make all other consumers report its shared classification without redefining exit policy.
